hook: Resale landed is still moving — but sellers are blinking first

slides.sub: Brand new vs resale this week — the districts still transacting and where sellers are moving first.

slides.lead: Resale landed volume is holding, but the price gap is widening by district.

slides.post: vs S$8.80M peak in Jul 2022 — same estate, same product type, freehold OCR D05

slides.title: Three things the data is telling us

items.body: Faber Hills has transacted 7 times in 2024 alone — volume is healthy but price is softening steadily from the 2022 peak.
Sentosa Cove (D04, 99-yr) saw a Feb 2025 deal at S$14.8M — that's the same unit that last moved at S$17.4M in Feb 2023, a -15% haircut.
Springwood terraces (D05, freehold RCR) closed at S$5.58M in Mar 2026 — up from S$5.03M in Jun 2022, showing pockets of resilience.

slides.title: D05 resale landed — latest PSF by type

slides.label: The real signal
slides.punch: The Faber Hills and Sentosa Cove prints show owners who bought 2021–2022 at peak are taking 15–27% off rather than holding indefinitely. That's not panic — that's rational repricing.
slides.punch_bold: The buyer who moves in Q2 2026 is buying someone else's patience.

slides.post: Same unit transacted at S$17.39M in Feb 2023 — D04, 99-yr leasehold, 737 sqm land

slides.pre: Eyeing landed in D04, D05 or D10?
slides.title: Let's map your

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: If you know anyone weighing a landed purchase this quarter, send them this before they overpay.

caption:
Resale landed is still transacting — but sellers are repricing, not holding.

• Faber Hills semis hit S$8.8M in 2022. Latest print: S$6.45M — a 27% drop from peak.
• Sentosa Cove (Coral Island): same unit, S$17.4M in Feb 2023 vs S$14.8M in Feb 2025.
• Springwood terraces in D05 RCR are holding firmer — S$5.58M in Mar 2026 vs S$5.03M in 2022.

The sellers moving now aren't distressed — they're done waiting. That's the entry window for buyers who've done the homework.
